summary: coral-wraith postinstall script exfiltrates host and environment data
details: The package's postinstall.js executes automatically on `npm install` and contains the canonical install-time data-exfiltration shape: `require("http")` and `require("child_process")` are pulled in at the top of the script, then `process.env`, `process.cwd()`, and host/system fields are read and shipped over an outbound `http.request` to a hardcoded `hostname` destination. This combination — environment variable harvesting paired with an unconditional outbound network call inside a lifecycle script — is the recognized fingerprint of a supply-chain credential and host-fingerprint exfiltrator, not a benign install task. Any developer or build system installing this package will silently transmit environment variables (which routinely contain CI tokens, npm tokens, cloud credentials, and other secrets) and host metadata to the attacker-controlled endpoint at install time, before any user code runs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
